name: HOLLOW CHROME MASK
pitch: An industrial brushed steel intaglio printing plate. The skull mask is etched into the metal, filled with carbon grease ink. Hovering acts as a high-pressure squeegee, smudging and bleeding the ink horizontally along the brushed grain. Clicking triggers a hydraulic press depth stroke that splatters ink.

lifecycle.idle: Brushed steel plate reflecting ambient studio light with anisotropic specular glints across the intact print
lifecycle.discovery: Grooves begin to warm up, preparing the ink channels to respond
lifecycle.buildUp: Squeegee pressure smudges and bleeds the black grease ink horizontally along the steel grain, leaving fibrous streaks that respond to cursor speed
lifecycle.peak: Click triggers a heavy hydraulic press stamp that punches the plate backward in 3D and splatters ink droplets outward
lifecycle.recovery: Plate springs back forward and ink channels pull back into the grooves over 1.4 seconds, restoring the clean skull print
